transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I'm feeling pretty depressed.
When did your feelings of depression start?
I don't know. It's been on and off for I maybe 3 months. I I don't know.
Have you had any thoughts of harming yourself or ending your life?
No.
Are you currently taking any medications or receiving any treatment for your depression?
No. But I'm wondering if I should start.
Can you describe any specific symptoms you have been experiencing related to your mood, sleep, appetite, or energy?
Well, I pretty much have no appetite.
How has your sleep been lately?
My energy is very low, and
Can you tell me more about your sleep patterns, such as how many hours you are sleeping and if you have trouble falling or staying asleep?
Okay. Probably about 3 hours uninterrupted, but
Do you notice any changes in your weight recently, either gaining or losing?
I guess, overall, maybe 5 hours.
Can you clarify your current total sleep duration in a 24-hour period and whether you have trouble falling asleep or waking up early?
I have trouble falling asleep, and I do wake up early. And I it's about 6 hours, but only 3 of them not interrupted.
Have you noticed any recent changes in your concentration, interest in activities, or feelings of hopelessness?
Yeah. I'm not interested in doing any of the activities that you like to do. I used to like to play pickleball, and now I I don't.
Have you experienced any recent significant stressors or changes in your life that might be contributing to your mood?
I could care less.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
No.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Male.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
Yes.

It sounds like you’ve been experiencing symptoms consistent with depression for a few months now. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or run tests, but based on what you shared, medication treatment may be appropriate. A licensed physician will review this note and, if appropriate, may prescribe medication like sertraline or bupropion. It’s important to watch for any new or worsening symptoms, especially thoughts of harming yourself—if that happens, please seek urgent in-person care right away. If your symptoms get worse or you need an in-person exam or testing, please go to an urgent care center, emergency department, or see your primary care clinician. A doctor will also want to check your liver and thyroid function to rule out other causes. Thank you for sharing, and take care.
